mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 8.0 percent, it signals a confirmed labor market inflection that reduces recession probabilities and compresses equity risk premiums, driving positive forward returns.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative or flat 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the cross-sectional predictive power drops below 0.02 IC over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Real economic expansion requires nominal GDP growth to outpace consumer price inflation. When GDPC1 growth exceeds CPIAUCSL growth over a rolling quarter, corporate profit margins expand, supporting positive equity returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term shows a negative coefficient in a multivariate regression over 5 years, or forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero when the spread widens.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery phases, a sequential rise in industrial production indicates rapidly easing supply bottlenecks and rising capacity utilization, which immediately translates into positive short-term equity momentum as earnings revisions turn upward.
failure_condition: INDPRO surges while S&P 500 5-day returns remain negative in over 60% of observations, indicating supply shocks are stagflationary rather than growth-positive.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts serve as a leading indicator for consumer confidence and construction sector momentum. When HOUST increases by more than 50 thousand units over a three-month window, it triggers a positive wealth effect that lifts equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: The threshold breach consistently precedes negative or flat 20-day equity returns, or the predictive accuracy falls below 52% in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Peak cycle conditions emerge when employment growth accelerates while monetary policy remains restrictive. The joint rise in PAYEMS and FEDFUNDS signals an overheating economy that will inevitably trigger a policy-induced slowdown, depressing equity valuations over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict negative returns in at least 55% of instances, or the interaction coefficient becomes positive during historical peak cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales directly measure consumer spending, which drives approximately 70 percent of US GDP. A persistent upward trend in RSAFS provides immediate positive momentum for corporate earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: The rolling correlation between RSAFS momentum and 5-day SPX returns remains below 0.10 over any 3-year period, or forward returns are negative in more than 50% of trending instances.

mechanism_narrative: In an inflationary tightening cycle, rising consumer prices combined with constrained federal funds rates compress real interest rates into negative territory. This erodes purchasing power and forces equity repricing, producing negative forward returns over a 20-day window.
failure_condition: Real rates turn negative while S&P 500 20-day returns remain positive in over 60% of cases, or the cross-sectional IC of the real rate spread exceeds 0.05 in the wrong direction.
